response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering expertise and experience with large-scale systems, they lack the core required domain knowledge for this role. The resume shows minimal Java and Kotlin experience with no Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate work, and only 6-month-old IDE tooling experience from 2014. Recent career trajectory has been toward TypeScript/Node.js full-stack work, moving away from the JVM and backend systems expertise required for this mid-senior IDE engineering position.
strengths: Proven ability to architect and maintain complex, large-scale systems handling millions of requests (Dunnhumby, Stripe, Shopify)
Strong software engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, clean architecture, mentoring, and open-source contributions
Prior IDE development experience with LabWindows/CVI at National Instruments demonstrates tooling familiarity
concerns: Minimal Java and Kotlin expertise—no demonstrated experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise Java frameworks despite listing Java in skills
Limited IDE internals and language tooling background; only 6 months of LabWindows work in 2014, nothing in modern language tools or IDEs
Recent career progression toward TypeScript/Node.js full-stack and AI infrastructure roles indicates movement away from JVM/backend systems domain

key_factors.factor: General software engineering skills and learning ability
